structured.name: Offline Authorization of unknown Id
structured.objective:
To allow automatic authorization of any "unknown" identifiers that cannot be explicitly authorized
by Authorization Cache entries.
structured.description:
This use case describes the scenario of presented "unknown" identifiers, other than are present in
an Authorization Cache or Local Cache entry using OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled.
scenario: 1. The EV Driver wants to start charging the EV and presents the IdToken.

2. The Charging Station checks the Local Authorization List, the IdToken is not present in the
Local Authorization List.

3. The Charging Station checks the Authorization Cache, the IdToken is not present in the
Authorization Cache.

4. The Charging Station accepts the unknown IdToken if OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is
set True

5. The Charging Station rejects the unknown IdToken if OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is
set False
structured.prerequisites:
The Charging Station is Offline.
Unknown IdToken presented (Not in the Authorization Cache and/or Local Authorization List).
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The authorization status in TransactionEventResponse, that is received once the Charging Station
is online, is Accepted.
Failure postcondition:
The authorization status in TransactionEventResponse that is received once the Charging Station
is online, is not Accepted, although OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is True.
structured.remarks:
This applies to all types of identifiers, including an eMAID that is presented as part of an ISO
15118 contract certificate.

If the identifier is authorized via
OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL NOT add the token to
Authorization Cache

requirements.precondition:
When connection to the CSMS is
restored
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a
TransactionEventRequest for any transaction that
was authorized offline.

requirements.precondition:
C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
transfer and send TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Updated) with triggerReason set to
Deauthorized and chargingState set to
SuspendedEVSE or preferably to EVConnected.

requirements.precondition:
C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop the transaction
and send TransactionEventRequest (eventType =
Ended) with triggerReason set to Deauthorized and
stoppedReason set to DeAuthorized.

requirements.precondition:
C15.FR.04 AND
If the Charging Station has the
possibility to lock the Charging Cable
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHOULD keep the Charging
Cable locked until the owner presents his identifier.

requirements.precondition:
C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is not
implemented or has been exceeded.
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
EnergyTransfer
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
delivery to the EV immediately and send
TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated)
with triggerReason set to ChargingStateChanged
and chargingState set to SuspendedEVSE

requirements.precondition:
C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is set and
has NOT been exceeded.
requirements.definition:
Energy delivery to the EV SHALL be allowed until
the amount of energy specified in
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId has been reached.

requirements.precondition:
When an unknown identifier is
presented AND
OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is set
to true
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL accept the presented
IdToken.

chunks.content:
# C15 - Offline Authorization of unknown Id

**Objective:** To allow automatic authorization of any "unknown" identifiers that cannot be explicitly authorized
by Authorization Cache entries.

**Description:** This use case describes the scenario of presented "unknown" identifiers, other than are present in
an Authorization Cache or Local Cache entry using OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled.

**Actors:** Charging Station, EV Driver

**Scenario:**
1. The EV Driver wants to start charging the EV and presents the IdToken.
2. The Charging Station checks the Local Authorization List, the IdToken is not present in the
Local Authorization List.
3. The Charging Station checks the Authorization Cache, the IdToken is not present in the
Authorization Cache.
4. The Charging Station accepts the unknown IdToken if OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is
set True
5. The Charging Station rejects the unknown IdToken if OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is
set False

**Prerequisites:** The Charging Station is Offline.
Unknown IdToken presented (Not in the Authorization Cache and/or Local Authorization List).

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The authorization status in TransactionEventResponse, that is received once the Charging Station
is online, is Accepted.
Failure postcondition:
The authorization status in TransactionEventResponse that is received once the Charging Station
is online, is not Accepted, although OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is True.

**Remarks:** This applies to all types of identifiers, including an eMAID that is presented as part of an ISO
15118 contract certificate.

metadata.heading: C15 - Offline Authorization of unknown Id

chunks.content:
# C15 - Requirements

**C15.FR.01**
Precondition: If the identifier is authorized via
OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled
The Charging Station SHALL NOT add the token to
Authorization Cache

**C15.FR.02**
Precondition: When connection to the CSMS is
restored
The Charging Station SHALL send a
TransactionEventRequest for any transaction that
was authorized offline.

**C15.FR.03**
Precondition: C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
transfer and send TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Updated) with triggerReason set to
Deauthorized and chargingState set to
SuspendedEVSE or preferably to EVConnected.

**C15.FR.04**
Precondition: C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
The Charging Station SHALL stop the transaction
and send TransactionEventRequest (eventType =
Ended) with triggerReason set to Deauthorized and
stoppedReason set to DeAuthorized.

**C15.FR.05**
Precondition: C15.FR.04 AND
If the Charging Station has the
possibility to lock the Charging Cable
The Charging Station SHOULD keep the Charging
Cable locked until the owner presents his identifier.

**C15.FR.06**
Precondition: C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is not
implemented or has been exceeded.
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
EnergyTransfer
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
delivery to the EV immediately and send
TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated)
with triggerReason set to ChargingStateChanged
and chargingState set to SuspendedEVSE

**C15.FR.07**
Precondition: C15.FR.02 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is set and
has NOT been exceeded.
Energy delivery to the EV SHALL be allowed until
the amount of energy specified in
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId has been reached.

**C15.FR.08**
Precondition: When an unknown identifier is
presented AND
OfflineTxForUnknownIdEnabled is set
to true
The Charging Station SHALL accept the presented
IdToken.
